The easiest way to see data fast is to use the PAX Vault: [https://pax-vault.f3nation.com/](https://pax-vault.f3nation.com/)

Beyond that you have to get access to the database views via Google BigQuery.

***NOTE:*** Each request to the Database using Big Query does cost $$ so make sure anything you write utilizing Looker Studio and BigQuery only queries as necessary.  By default looker studio has it refresh data every 12 hours which should be acceptable.  *Please do not over query for data and drive up costs.*

**Requesting access to F3Data.Analytics from Google Big Query**

1) As the user you are going to write your queries/looker studio dashboards in you need to request access to this Google Group: [https://groups.google.com/a/f3nation.com/g/f3-data-analytics](https://groups.google.com/a/f3nation.com/g/f3-data-analytics)   
   1) If your region has a shared gmail account you may want to use that one instead of your personal but that is up to you  
2) If you have questions check this thread on F3 Nation: [https://f3nation.slack.com/archives/C01HCL3VBEG/p1765117005773029](https://f3nation.slack.com/archives/C01HCL3VBEG/p1765117005773029)  
3) You can verify access by going into the console and testing a query; [https://console.cloud.google.com/bigquery?referrer=search\&project=f3data](https://console.cloud.google.com/bigquery?referrer=search&project=f3data)  
4) You should be able to navigate Studio \-\> f3data \-\> analytics and see these tables like these  
   ![][image1]

**Shared Queries**  
There are some shared Queries to help you get started with ideas if you need under the Queries \-\> Shared Queries section.![][image2]

**Writing Queries**  
One of the best ways I have found to do this is to take screenshots of all the tables under the analytics section and put those in my Google Drive folder.    
Here are those files:   
[event\_info3.png](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1dhoiGXqCSlphOmICqR30WkyqB1SXugoV&usp=drive_copy)  
[attendance\_info.png](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1EHSCoP04pVbunMGYcArPIJJIpOPl2n_m&usp=drive_copy)  
[event\_info2.png](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1B7LdI1qKh0HEgLK_n_gas47LqqwK75Dz&usp=drive_copy)  
[Event\_info1.png](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1BtqO0LZgCKsZrj5wTGu6DbNVROt853jS&usp=drive_copy)

Then I have a Google Doc with this saved text as my prompt to use in Google Gemini Pro (which has access to my Google Drive).  Maybe sure you replace \<QUERY\> with what it is you want the query to find and specify what sort of output you are looking for.  And delete the Looker studio line if you don’t plan on putting it in there;

Using the screenshots of the f3data.analytics bigquery database views that I have access to I want your help to craft a Google Big Query to \<QUERY\>

I also want to put this into a Looker Studio Dashboard what are the steps for that and do I need a slightly different query for that.

In my Google Drive the screenshots are in a folder called “F3 Stuff” and they are called;   
attendance\_info.png  
event\_info1.png  
event\_info2.png  
Event\_info3.png

**Using BigQuery in Looker Studio**

**Method 1: Copy existing dashboards**  
If you just want dashboards you can copy and modify then you can look at [https://f3naperville.com/stats/](https://f3naperville.com/stats/) and any of the ones on there you like you should be able to copy to your personal looker studio space and then edit the query to fit your region or settings as needed.  
To edit the query for the ‘Event Attendance Report’ (  
1\) Open the Looker Studio Dashboard in Edit mode  
2\) Go to the Resource Menu \-\> Manage added data sources  
![][image3]  
3\) Click the ‘Edit’ action for the BigQuery connector type  
![][image4]  
4\) Then click “Edit Connection”  
![][image5]  
5\) Now you can edit the query and change the region from “Naperville” to your region  
![][image6]  
6\) Click the ‘Reconnect’ button in the upper right and go back to editing the Looker Studio dashboard and you should have your regions data.

**Method 2: Creating a dashboard from scratch**

1) First you should create a query using Google Biq Query as show in the section above (and helps that you tell the AI you want to do it in looker studio it will even give you the steps for building any filters and things)  
2) In Looker Studio start a Blank Report and select “Big Query” as your datasource  
3) Click “CUSTOM QUERY”  
4) Click “Data”  
5) Now you can paste in your query that you made in Big Query and then click the Add button in the lower right  
   ![][image7]  
6) At this point it takes you back to the Looker Studio dashboard design view and you can get started on designing.  I would recommend the responsive layout if you want it to be viewable on computers and mobile phones with less work from you.
